questions.question: Is creating a database using Notion AI free?

options.text: Yes, it is completely free to use.
options.explanation: Creating a database with Notion AI is free; you do not need a Notion AI subscription for your workspace.

options.text: No, it requires a paid Notion AI subscription.
options.explanation: This is incorrect. You can create databases with Notion AI without having Notion AI for your workspace.

options.text: No, it counts toward your complimentary AI responses.
options.explanation: Creating a database with Notion AI does not count toward your complimentary AI responses.

options.text: It depends on the type of database you create.
options.explanation: The cost is not dependent on the database type; it is free to create any database with Notion AI.

questions.question: Which of the following limitations applies to databases created with Notion AI?

options.text: It cannot edit existing databases.
options.explanation: Notion AI can create new databases but cannot edit existing databases.

options.text: It cannot create pages within a database.
options.explanation: Notion AI cannot create pages in your database, database automations, forms, charts, or templates.

options.text: It cannot visualize databases as boards or calendars.
options.explanation: Notion AI allows you to choose how you prefer to visualize the database items (e.g., List, Board, Calendar) before selecting Done.

options.text: It cannot be moved to another page.
options.explanation: You can move databases created by AI to another page in your workspace.

questions.question: When setting up AI Autofill for a Text property, which option is available?

options.text: Custom autofill: AI generates content based on your custom prompt.
options.explanation: Custom autofill is a valid option in the menu next to 'Fill with' for Text properties.

options.text: Generate new options: AI creates new tags for the property.
options.explanation: The 'Generate new options' feature is specific to Select and Multi-select properties, not Text.

options.text: Auto-update: Updates the property 5 minutes after changes.
options.explanation: Auto-update is a setting found under 'More' in Select/Multi-select properties, not a 'Fill with' option for Text.

options.text: Web Search: AI reads information on the internet.
options.explanation: Notion AI Autofill does not read information on the web.

questions.question: How can you manually trigger AI to fill or update a Select or Multi-select property?

options.text: Click the wand button that appears when hovering over a value.
options.explanation: You can manually trigger AI by hovering over an existing value and clicking the wand button that appears.

options.text: Select 'More' and choose 'Trigger AI'.
options.explanation: While there is an 'Auto-update' option under 'More', there is no direct 'Trigger AI' menu item; the wand button is the manual method.

options.text: Wait for the 5-minute auto-update window.
options.explanation: The 5-minute window applies only when 'Auto-update on page edits' is enabled; you can manually trigger it immediately using the wand.

options.text: Edit the page title to force an update.
options.explanation: Editing the title does not trigger AI Autofill for Select/Multi-select properties.

questions.question: What happens if you toggle on 'Generate new options' for a Select or Multi-select property?

options.text: AI will automatically assign these new options to all existing database entries.
options.explanation: Turning this on allows AI to generate new options to apply to *new* database entries, not automatically to existing ones.

options.text: AI will create a new database property for you.
options.explanation: This toggle only affects the options available for the existing Select or Multi-select property.

options.text: AI will generate new options to apply to any new database entries.
options.explanation: When this is turned on, AI will generate new options to apply to any new database entries.

options.text: You must manually type the options into the property.
options.explanation: The purpose of the toggle is to automate the generation of options using AI.

questions.question: If you use the Home widget to build a database with Notion AI, where is the database located?

options.text: It is added to your workspace as a new page.
options.explanation: Databases created with Build mode in Home are added to your workspace as a new page.

options.text: It is added to the 'Home' view within the widget.
options.explanation: The Home widget is used to create the database; the resulting database is a standalone page in the workspace.

options.text: It is embedded into the Home page itself.
options.explanation: It is not embedded into the Home page; it is a new page added to the workspace.

options.text: It is only visible when you toggle to Build mode again.
options.explanation: Once created, the database exists as a standard page in your workspace.

questions.question: You want to build a database inside a specific, existing page. How do you do this?

options.text: Navigate to that specific page and use the slash command /database.
options.explanation: To build a database in a specific page, you must navigate to that page and create the database there using the slash command.

options.text: Use the Home widget and specify the page name in the prompt.
options.explanation: Using the Home widget will create a new page in the workspace, not insert a database into a specific existing page.

options.text: Create the database in Home and then drag and drop it into the page.
options.explanation: While you can move a database created by AI to another page afterwards, the prompt asks how to build *in* a specific page initially.

options.text: Use the slash command /autofill in the Home page.
options.explanation: There is no '/autofill' slash command. The correct command is '/database'.

questions.question: What is the recommended practice regarding the accuracy of Notion AI's responses?

options.text: You should not rely on Notion AI for critical content.
options.explanation: While Writer is effective, you should always check the accuracy of content from Notion AI before relying on it for critical content.

options.text: You should always verify the accuracy of content before using it for critical tasks.
options.explanation: It is recommended to always check the accuracy of content from Notion AI before relying on it for critical content.

options.text: Notion AI is 100% accurate for all database operations.
options.explanation: There are no guarantees of 100% accuracy, so verification is necessary.

options.text: You should use Notion AI only for brainstorming to avoid errors.
options.explanation: Notion AI is effective for summarizing and extracting, but accuracy must still be verified regardless of the use case.

questions.question: Which property type has a specific limitation regarding text files?

options.text: Text property
options.explanation: The limitation applies to the File property, not the Text property.

options.text: Select property
options.explanation: The limitation applies to the File property, not the Select property.

options.text: File property
options.explanation: Notion AI Autofill cannot reference text files attached in a File property.

options.text: Multi-select property
options.explanation: The limitation applies to the File property, not the Multi-select property.

questions.question: How long does the 'Auto-update on page edits' setting wait before updating a Select or Multi-select property?

options.text: Immediately
options.explanation: The property updates five minutes after changes are made, not immediately.

options.text: After 5 minutes
options.explanation: When 'Auto-update on page edits' is enabled, the property updates five minutes after changes are made to the page.

options.text: After 10 minutes
options.explanation: The specified wait time is 5 minutes, not 10.

options.text: After page refresh
options.explanation: The update is time-based (5 minutes), not triggered by a page refresh.

questions.question: Can Notion AI Autofill read information from the internet?

options.text: Yes, it has real-time web access.
options.explanation: No, Notion AI Autofill does not read information on the web.

options.text: No, it only processes data within your workspace.
options.explanation: Notion AI Autofill does not read information on the web; it processes data within your workspace.

options.text: Only if you explicitly ask it to search.
options.explanation: There is no web search capability in the standard Autofill feature.

options.text: Yes, for specific properties like Select.
options.explanation: This is a general limitation; it applies to all property types.
